_instructions: Copy this file to confluence_request_profile.json on the Confluence-connected machine.
Replace only fields required by the confirmed working read-only requests; this is configuration, not Python code.
Do not put a hostname, page ID, space key, username, PAT, cookie, or Authorization value in this file.
Paths are exact origin-absolute HTTP request paths and must include any Confluence context path.
Keep {root_page_id}, {space_key}, and {page_size} as literal runtime placeholders where needed.
Never configure body, attachment, comment, restriction, ACL/permission, rendered HTML, download, or export resources.
For pagination.kind use exactly json_next, link_header, cursor_value, or start_limit. Unused pagination fields may remain unchanged.

For json_next/link_header, mutable_query_parameters lists only pagination-position keys that the confirmed next URL may add/change; selector, type/status, parent/ancestor, and page-size keys are never mutable.
The probe accepts reordered immutable query pairs but rejects any immutable name/value/count change.
For cursor_value, next_pointer locates the opaque response cursor and cursor_query_parameter is the confirmed request query name.
For start_limit, replace inventory_request.query with the two-pair _start_limit_inventory_query_example and make its names exactly match the pagination parameter names.
